content_en: Then he went to the other fortresses and citadels and took control of them, so he gained control of the entire region of Yemen, and gave sermons in the name of the Abbasid caliph.756 He killed the impostor who was called 'Abd an-Nabi, and all turmoil ceased in Yemen, and the country went back to the way it used to be.757 Shams adDawlah wrote to his brother al-Malik an-Nasir Salah ad-Deen, telling him of the conquests that Allah had granted him and the favour that He had bestowed upon him. Salah ad-Deen wrote to Noor ad-Deen telling him the news, and Noor ad-Deen wrote in turn to the caliph, telling him the good news that Yemen had been conquered and sermons were now being delivered in his name there.758 Conquest of Nubia At that time, Nubia was a Christian kingdom; its capital was the city of Danqalah which was located at the headwaters of the Nile. It had had a strong connection to Egypt since the Islamic conquest. When the Ayubid state was established in Egypt, Salah ad-Deen wanted to conquer Nubia in order to protect Egypt from any aggression coming from the south. He sent his brother Turanshah, in Jumada II 568 AH/January 1173 CE to Nubia. He conquered Ibreem, capturing prisoners and booty, then he went back to Qaws, and Islam entered regions where the Muslim army had never been before. He appointed Ibraheem al-Kurdi as its governor.759 This conquest removed the barriers that had been preventing the spread of Islam in that region.760 The real story behind the alienation between Salah ad-Deen and Noor ad-Deen Historians have discussed the relationship between Noor ad-Deen and Salah ad-Deen. Ibn al-Atheer narrated the reasons for the alienation between Noor ad-Deen and Salah ad-Deen, as did Abu Shamah, quoting from Ibn Abi Tay\ It started in 567 AH, when they agreed to besiege Kerak, but Salah adDeen went back to Egypt before meeting Noor ad-Deen.761 A number of other historians762 narrated from Ibn al-Atheer and Ibn Abi Tay', and they were followed in turn by some modern historians, without checking the facts, who went to extremes in explaining the reasons for this alienation and its consequences. They described the relationship between Noor ad-Deen and Salah ad-Deen as if it were a hostile relationship in which each feared the other, so Salah ad-Deen started trying to get rid of Noor ad-Deen's authority and he preferred to leave Kerak as a buffer zone between him and Noor ad- Deen; Noor ad-Deen also thought that he had made a mistake by sending Asad ad-Deen and Salah ad-Deen to Egypt. Noor ad-Deen is described as a serious opponent to Salah ad-Deen, and so on.763 This false analysis has no basis except the reports of Ibn Abi Tay' and Ibn al-Atheer: As for Ibn Abi Tay he tried, using the skills of fabrication and lying in which he was proficient, to discredit the relationship between the two men. He is to be regarded with suspicion when it comes to what he attributed to Noor ad-Deen of unbecoming attitudes and deeds. Noor ad-Deen had humiliated the Shiites in Aleppo, abolished their symbols and made Ahl alSunnah stronger. The father of Abu Tay' was one of the Shiite leaders who had been banished from Aleppo. Hence we see that Ibn Abi Tay' was very biased against Noor ad-Deen and he tried to distort the relationship between these two great men with his lies.764 As for Ibn al-Atheer, he is to be regarded with suspicion when it comes to what he wrote about Salah ad-Deen. He sometimes looked for opportunities to criticize Salah ad-Deen and undermine his reputation, especially when comparing him with Noor ad-Deen.765 In his books Al-Kamil fit-Tareekh and Al-Bahirfee Tdreekh ad-Dawlah al-Atabekiyah, the historian of the Zangid dynasty mentions his views concerning this issue, and this was quoted from him by a number of historians. He concluded that Salah ad-Deen was not sincere to his master Noor ad-Deen; rather, from the time he established his authority in Egypt, he was striving to become independent of him, and he was competing with him for political dominance in Syria. All of these opinions766 were written by Ibn al-Atheer after the death of Salah adDeen. Salah ad-Deen had to go out to Syria at the head of his troops and annex the possessions of his master Noor ad-Deen to his own possessions in Egypt, because Salah ad-Deen's going out to Syria was aimed at reuniting the Islamic front, which was the aim which Imad ad-Deen Zangi and his son Noor ad-Deen had themselves striven to achieve for a long time. After the death of Noor ad-Deen, the Islamic front was about to split and the situation would have gone back to the way it was before, namely division and weakness, after the division of the Zangids into two factions, one in Damascus and the other in Aleppo. His son, at-Tifl as-Salih Isma'eel, was not able to reunite the kingdom of his father.767 Salah ad-Deen wrote to the Abbasid caliph and to the son of Noor ad-Deen, telling them that he was setting out for Syria in order to unite the Muslims against the Franks.
content_id: Kemudian ia pergi ke benteng-benteng dan citadel lainnya dan mengambil alih kendali mereka, sehingga ia menguasai seluruh wilayah Yaman, dan menyampaikan khutbah atas nama Khalifah Abbasiyah.756 Ia membunuh penipu yang disebut 'Abd an-Nabi, dan semua kekacauan mereda di Yaman, dan negeri itu kembali ke keadaan semula.757 Syams ad-Dawlah menulis surat kepada saudaranya al-Malik an-Nasir Salah ad-Deen, memberitahunya tentang penaklukan-penaklukan yang Allah anugerahkan kepadanya dan karunia yang Ia limpahkan kepadanya. Salah ad-Deen menulis kepada Noor ad-Deen memberitahukan beritanya, dan Noor ad-Deen pada gilirannya menulis kepada khalifah memberitahukannya kabar gembira bahwa Yaman telah ditaklukkan dan khutbah kini disampaikan atas namanya di sana.758 Penaklukan Nubia. Pada saat itu Nubia adalah kerajaan Kristen; ibu kotanya adalah kota Danqalah yang terletak di hulu Sungai Nil. Ia telah memiliki hubungan yang kuat dengan Mesir sejak penaklukan Islam. Ketika negara Ayyubid didirikan di Mesir, Salah ad-Deen ingin menaklukkan Nubia untuk melindungi Mesir dari agresi yang datang dari selatan. Ia mengirimkan saudaranya Turanshah, pada Jumadah II 568 H/Januari 1173 M ke Nubia. Ia menaklukkan Ibreem, menangkap tawanan dan rampasan perang, kemudian kembali ke Qaws, dan Islam memasuki daerah-daerah di mana pasukan Muslim tidak pernah menginjakkan kaki sebelumnya. Ia menunjuk Ibrahim al-Kurdi sebagai gubernurnya.759 Penaklukan ini menghilangkan hambatan-hambatan yang telah mencegah penyebaran Islam di wilayah itu.760 Kisah nyata di balik perselisihan antara Salah ad-Deen dan Noor ad-Deen. Para sejarawan telah membahas hubungan antara Noor ad-Deen dan Salah ad-Deen. Ibn al-Atheer meriwayatkan alasan-alasan perselisihan antara Noor ad-Deen dan Salah ad-Deen, demikian pula Abu Syamah, mengutip dari Ibn Abi Tay'. Dimulai pada 567 H, ketika mereka sepakat untuk mengepung Kerak, tetapi Salah ad-Deen kembali ke Mesir sebelum bertemu Noor ad-Deen.761 Sejumlah sejarawan lain762 meriwayatkan dari Ibn al-Atheer dan Ibn Abi Tay', dan mereka diikuti oleh beberapa sejarawan modern, tanpa memeriksa fakta-fakta, yang berlebihan dalam menjelaskan alasan-alasan perselisihan ini dan konsekuensinya. Mereka menggambarkan hubungan antara Noor ad-Deen dan Salah ad-Deen seolah-olah merupakan hubungan permusuhan di mana masing-masing takut kepada yang lain, sehingga Salah ad-Deen mulai berusaha menyingkirkan otoritas Noor ad-Deen dan ia lebih memilih membiarkan Kerak sebagai zona penyangga antara dirinya dan Noor ad-Deen; Noor ad-Deen juga berpikir bahwa ia telah membuat kesalahan dengan mengirimkan Asad ad-Deen dan Salah ad-Deen ke Mesir. Noor ad-Deen digambarkan sebagai lawan serius Salah ad-Deen, dan seterusnya.763 Analisis palsu ini tidak memiliki dasar selain laporan-laporan Ibn Abi Tay' dan Ibn al-Atheer: Adapun Ibn Abi Tay', ia berusaha, menggunakan keterampilan pemalsuan dan kebohongan yang ia kuasai, untuk mendiskreditkan hubungan antara kedua orang tersebut. Ia harus dipandang dengan kecurigaan ketika menyangkut apa yang ia nisbatkan kepada Noor ad-Deen berupa sikap dan perbuatan yang tidak pantas. Noor ad-Deen telah menghinakan kaum Syi'ah di Aleppo, menghapus simbol-simbol mereka, dan memperkuat Ahlus Sunnah. Ayah Abu Tay' adalah salah satu pemimpin Syi'ah yang telah diusir dari Aleppo. Oleh karena itu kita melihat bahwa Ibn Abi Tay' sangat berprasangka terhadap Noor ad-Deen dan ia berusaha mendistorsi hubungan antara dua orang besar ini dengan kebohongannya.764 Adapun Ibn al-Atheer, ia harus dipandang dengan kecurigaan ketika menyangkut apa yang ia tulis tentang Salah ad-Deen. Ia kadang-kadang mencari kesempatan untuk mengkritik Salah ad-Deen dan merusak reputasinya, terutama ketika membandingkannya dengan Noor ad-Deen.765 Dalam bukunya Al-Kamil fit-Tarikh dan Al-Bahir fi Tarikh ad-Dawlah al-Atabekiyah, sejarawan dinasti Zangid menyebutkan pandangannya tentang masalah ini, dan ini dikutip darinya oleh sejumlah sejarawan. Ia menyimpulkan bahwa Salah ad-Deen tidak tulus kepada tuannya Noor ad-Deen; sebaliknya, sejak ia mendirikan otoritasnya di Mesir, ia berjuang untuk menjadi mandiri darinya, dan ia bersaing dengannya untuk dominasi politik di Suriah. Semua pendapat ini766 ditulis oleh Ibn al-Atheer setelah kematian Salah ad-Deen. Salah ad-Deen harus pergi ke Suriah memimpin pasukannya dan menganeksasi kepemilikan tuannya Noor ad-Deen ke kepemilikannya sendiri di Mesir, karena kepergian Salah ad-Deen ke Suriah bertujuan menyatukan kembali front Islam, yang merupakan tujuan yang telah Imad ad-Deen Zangi dan putranya Noor ad-Deen sendiri berupaya capai untuk waktu yang lama. Setelah kematian Noor ad-Deen, front Islam hampir terpecah dan keadaan akan kembali seperti semula, yaitu perpecahan dan kelemahan, setelah perpecahan kaum Zangid menjadi dua faksi, satu di Damaskus dan yang lainnya di Aleppo. Putranya, at-Tifl as-Salih Isma'eel, tidak mampu menyatukan kembali kerajaan ayahnya.767 Salah ad-Deen menulis kepada Khalifah Abbasiyah dan kepada putra Noor ad-Deen, memberitahukan mereka bahwa ia berangkat ke Suriah untuk menyatukan kaum Muslimin melawan kaum Frank.
